metadata.title: SpyCloud Darknet & Identity Threat Exposure Intelligence for Microsoft Sentinel
metadata.description: Complete deployment of SpyCloud Darknet & Identity Threat Exposure Intelligence for Microsoft Sentinel v10.0. Dual-DCR architecture (primary: 10 streams, extended: 4 streams) to comply with Azure 10-dataFlow-per-DCR limit. Includes: CCF data connector (13 REST API pollers covering Enterprise Breach, Compass, SIP, Investigations, IDLINK, Data Partnership, Exposure, and CAP APIs), 14 custom Log Analytics tables, playbook templates (19 remediation playbooks), analytics rule templates (60+ rules), Fusion multistage ML detection, MSIC rules, UEBA/Anomaly settings, automation rule templates, 28 hunting queries, 4 watchlists, 14 focused workbooks, TI enrichment, Jupyter notebooks, and full Security Copilot integration (120+ KQL skills + 35+ API skills + 23 sub-agent investigation agent with 12 GPT-4o AI analysis skills).
metadata.author: SpyCloud

metadata.description: PRIMARY AZURE REGION — This single selection determines the region for ALL deployed resources: the Log Analytics workspace, Data Collection Endpoint (DCE), Data Collection Rule (DCR), custom tables, and all Sentinel solution artifacts. Defaults to your resource group's region. Only change this if you need resources in a different region than your resource group. IMPORTANT: If you have an existing Log Analytics workspace, select the region where it is already deployed. The Azure Portal will dynamically show all available regions for your subscription — no hardcoded list needed.

OPTIONAL — Override the workspace region if it differs from the primary deployment region above. Leave BLANK to use the primary deployment region for everything (recommended for most deployments). Only set this if your Log Analytics workspace is in a different region than your resource group. When set, all workspace-scoped resources (tables, connectors, DCR, DCE) will deploy to this region instead.

Azure Subscription ID where Microsoft Sentinel is deployed. Auto-detected from your current context. Only override this for cross-subscription deployments where Sentinel is in a different subscription than where you are deploying from.

Resource group containing the Microsoft Sentinel workspace. Auto-detected from your current context. All SpyCloud resources (DCE, DCR, custom tables, connectors) will be deployed into this resource group. Only override if your Sentinel workspace is in a different resource group.

REQUIRED — Name of the Log Analytics workspace for Microsoft Sentinel. If createNewWorkspace is TRUE: enter your desired name (e.g., 'spycloud-supreme-sentinel'). If createNewWorkspace is FALSE: enter the exact name of your existing workspace. To find existing workspaces: Azure Portal → Log Analytics workspaces, or CLI: az monitor log-analytics workspace list --query '[].name' -o tsv

Name for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) that receives SpyCloud API data. Default: 'dce-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCE is the HTTPS ingestion endpoint that the SpyCloud connector sends data to before it reaches your workspace. If a DCE with this name already exists in your resource group, it will be reused. Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCE will be 'dce-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Name for the Data Collection Rule (DCR) that transforms and routes SpyCloud data to the correct tables. Default: 'dcr-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCR contains KQL transforms that normalize incoming SpyCloud API data and route it to the appropriate custom table (Watchlist, Breach Catalog, MDE Logs, or Conditional Access Logs). Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCR will be 'dcr-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Number of days to retain SpyCloud data in Log Analytics custom tables. Applies to ALL four SpyCloud tables. • 30 days = Minimum retention (GDPR-friendly, lowest storage cost) • 60 days = Short-term investigation window • 90 days = RECOMMENDED — supports most SOC investigation workflows and SOC2/NIST baselines • 180 days = Extended — trend analysis, recurring breach pattern detection • 365 days = Full year — required for PCI DSS, annual SOC2 audits, some cyber insurance policies • 730 days = Maximum (2 years) — legal hold, regulatory investigations, litigation support COST IMPACT: Each additional 30 days adds ~1x your monthly ingestion volume to storage costs. For HIPAA (6-year requirement), use 730 days here plus Azure Log Analytics archive/export for long-term.

Azure resource tags applied to ALL deployed resources (DCE, DCR, tables, connectors). Use tags for cost tracking, environment labeling, compliance labeling, and resource governance. Default tags include solution name, vendor, data classification, and management method. Add your organization's required tags (costCenter, owner, project, department) as key-value pairs. Example: Add 'costCenter': 'SEC-OPS-001' and 'owner': 'soc-team@company.com' for chargebacks.

Pricing tier of your Log Analytics workspace (informational — does not change your workspace tier). • PerGB2018 = Pay-As-You-Go (most common, ~$2.76/GB ingested) • CapacityReservation = Committed tier with discounts (100GB/day+, saves 10-25%) • Free = Limited to 500MB/day ingestion (not recommended for SpyCloud) Typical SpyCloud ingestion: 1-50 MB/day (small), 50-500 MB/day (large enterprise). Check Azure Portal → Log Analytics → Usage and estimated costs for your current tier.

Public network access setting for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE). • 'Enabled' (DEFAULT) = DCE accepts data from the public internet (standard for cloud-to-cloud connectors) • 'Disabled' = DCE only accepts data via Private Link (requires Azure Private Link configuration) For most SpyCloud deployments, 'Enabled' is correct because the Sentinel CCF connector sends data from Azure's internal infrastructure to the DCE. Only set to 'Disabled' if your security policy mandates Private Link for all Monitor ingestion.
Auto-generated deployment timestamp in UTC. Do not modify — ARM populates this automatically at deployment time.
Deploy MDE Device Isolation playbook. REQUIRES: Defender for Endpoint P1/P2. Without license, deploys but cannot function.
Deploy CA Identity Protection playbook. REQUIRES: Entra ID P1/P2. Without license, deploys but cannot reset passwords.
Deploy the SpyCloud Infostealer Detection analytics rule. Creates Sentinel incidents when severity >= 20 exposures are detected.
Deploy the automation rule that connects the analytics rule to the playbooks for automatic remediation on incident creation.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group for blocked users (severity 25). Targeted by a CA policy that blocks all access. Create in Entra ID > Groups > New Group > Security. Leave blank to skip.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group for restricted users (severity 20). Targeted by a CA policy requiring compliant device + MFA. Create in Entra ID > Groups > New Group > Security. Leave blank to skip.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group for monitored users (severity 5+). Targeted by a CA policy requiring MFA from untrusted locations. Create in Entra ID > Groups > New Group > Security. Leave blank to skip.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group to add compromised users to. This group should be targeted by a Conditional Access policy that restricts access. Create the group first: Entra ID → Groups → New group → Security. Leave blank to skip CA group assignment.
Device isolation type for MDE remediation. 'Full' = complete network isolation (recommended for infostealer response). 'Selective' = allow Outlook, Teams, and Defender communication.
Tag name applied to compromised devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint. Used for tracking and filtering in the MDE portal.
Severity level assigned to Sentinel incidents created by the SpyCloud infostealer analytics rule.
How often the analytics rule runs. PT1H = every hour (recommended). PT5M = every 5 minutes (near real-time, higher cost).
Minimum SpyCloud severity to trigger remediation. 20 = infostealer credentials (recommended). 2 = all breaches (high volume). 25 = infostealer + stolen sessions only.
Create an Azure Monitor Action Group for email/webhook notifications when SpyCloud incidents are created or data ingestion stops.
OPTIONAL — Email address to receive alert notifications for SpyCloud incidents and data health issues. Required if enableNotifications is TRUE.
OPTIONAL — Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for alert notifications. Create one in Teams: Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys the full library of 49 SpyCloud analytics rules covering infostealer detection, plaintext password alerts, PII/SSN exposure, session cookie theft, device reinfection, credential reuse, geographic anomalies, VIP exposure, and remediation gap detection. Rules deploy ENABLED by default with recommended settings. Disable individual rules post-deployment in Sentinel > Analytics if needed.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys rules for detecting stolen session cookies and tokens (severity 25). These are the most critical exposures as they enable MFA bypass via session hijacking, requiring immediate session revocation and device isolation.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys rules correlating SpyCloud exposures with third-party identity provider logs (Okta, Duo, Ping Identity, CyberArk). Detects compromised credentials being used across IdP sign-in events. Rules auto-skip gracefully if the required IdP connector is not installed.

Set to TRUE to create a new Log Analytics workspace AND enable Microsoft Sentinel on it. Set to FALSE if you already have a workspace with Sentinel enabled. When TRUE, a new workspace is created using the name in the 'workspace' parameter.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Immutable ID for the extended DCR (dcr-ccf-ext-*) used by Exposure, CAP, MDE, and CA Logs streams. Auto-resolved by post-deploy.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — After ARM deployment, run scripts/post-deploy-auto.sh to automatically resolve DCE/DCR, assign RBAC roles, grant managed identity permissions, grant admin consent, enable rules, and verify deployment.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Credential response playbook with password reset + Teams alert notification. Combines identity remediation with SOC awareness. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2 for password reset; Teams notifications work without additional license.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Scheduled MDE blocklist scan that auto-isolates devices matching severity 25 infostealer records. Runs on configurable schedule (default: every 4 hours). Requires: MDE P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for SOC alert notifications. Used by the Credential Response playbook to send real-time alerts to your security operations channel. Leave blank to skip Teams integration.
ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.service-now.com) for automatic ticket creation. Leave blank to skip ServiceNow integration.
How often (in hours) the MDE Blocklist playbook runs to scan for new severity 25 infostealer matches against Defender for Endpoint. Default: every 4 hours.

Deploy the SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard workbook in Sentinel. Provides real-time charts for exposure overview, severity trends, top users, infected devices, password analysis, remediation status, and breach catalog.

Enable User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) in Microsoft Sentinel. UEBA identifies anomalous behavior by building behavioral profiles of users and entities. Required for SpyCloud analytics rule #29 (UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User). Enables: Entra ID entity provider, SigninLogs, AuditLogs, AzureActivity data sources.

Enable built-in Anomaly detection rules in Microsoft Sentinel. ML-powered anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns in sign-in behavior, resource access, and network activity. Complements SpyCloud credential exposure alerts with behavioral context.

WARNING: Set to FALSE if Fusion is already enabled in your workspace (default). The Fusion template can only be installed ONCE per workspace. Only set to true on a brand-new Sentinel workspace where Fusion has never been enabled.

Deploy Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules from Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, and MCAS. Set to true only on first deployment - these built-in rules require ETag for updates and cannot be redeployed.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Enriches incidents with VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, and GreyNoise threat intelligence lookups for IPs found in infostealer data. Free tier API keys supported. If no VirusTotal API key is provided, the playbook deploys but skips TI lookups until configured.
VirusTotal API key for the TI Enrichment playbook. Free tier allows 4 requests/minute. Get yours at: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey
Environment tag applied to all resources.
Owner tag — team or individual responsible.
Cost center tag for billing.
Pricing tier for new Log Analytics workspace (only when createNewWorkspace=TRUE). PerGB2018=Pay-As-You-Go, CapacityReservation=committed discounts.

Slack Incoming Webhook URL for SOC notifications. Logic Apps will send alerts here when incidents are created. Leave blank to skip Slack integration. Create at: api.slack.com/apps → Incoming Webhooks.
Jira Automation webhook URL for auto-creating issues on credential exposure. Leave blank to skip.
Azure DevOps Service Hook URL for auto-creating work items. Leave blank to skip.
Deployment mode: Full (first install), Upgrade (add features), or selective component deployment.
Deploy core SpyCloud detection analytics rules sc-001 through sc-012. These are the foundational infostealer and credential exposure detection rules recommended for all deployments.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys O365 & Entra ID correlation rules (sc-020 to sc-029). Correlates SpyCloud exposures with sign-in activity, impossible travel, mailbox rules, OAuth consent, and admin role changes. Requires: Entra ID and Office 365 connectors. Rules auto-skip if data sources are not connected.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys UEBA & firewall correlation rules (sc-030 to sc-039). Detects anomalous behavior, lateral movement, DNS C2 communication, and VPN anomalies from exposed users. Requires: UEBA enabled + firewall/CEF connector. Rules auto-skip if data sources are not connected.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys advanced threat detection rules (sc-040 to sc-049). Detects multi-stage attack chains, CA bypass attempts, credential re-exposure after reset, TI indicator matches, and geographic mismatches. Uses multiple data sources with graceful fallback.

Deploy 5 Microsoft Security Incident Creation (MSIC) rules that auto-create Sentinel incidents from Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, Microsoft Cloud App Security, and Azure Active Directory Identity Protection alerts.

Deploy the Fusion Advanced Multistage Attack Detection rule. Uses ML to correlate low-fidelity alerts across multiple data sources into high-confidence multi-stage attack incidents spanning the kill chain.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Forces password change on next sign-in via Microsoft Graph API when credentials are exposed. The user must re-authenticate with MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: run scripts/post-deploy-auto.sh to auto-grant User.ReadWrite.All permission.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Instantly revokes ALL active sessions (browser, app, refresh tokens) for compromised users. Critical for infostealer response where session cookies may be stolen. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deletes compromised MFA methods and forces re-registration. Prevents attackers from using stolen authenticator tokens or phone-based MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Adds compromised users to severity-tiered Conditional Access security groups (Blocked/Restricted/Monitored). Sev 25 → block all access, Sev 20 → require compliant device, Sev 5+ → require MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2 + pre-created security groups. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Automatically pushes malicious IP block rules to Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto PAN-OS, or Azure Firewall from SpyCloud infostealer C2 data. Post-deploy: configure firewall API URLs and keys in the Logic App designer. Deploys but remains inactive until firewall credentials are provided.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Isolates infected devices via MDE API. Sev 25 → full isolation (complete disconnect), Sev 20 → selective isolation (allows Outlook/Teams/Defender). Tags device as 'SpyCloud-Compromised' in MDE. Requires: MDE P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Emails the exposed user with breach details, required actions (change password, review sign-ins), and support contacts. Uses Office 365 Mail.Send via managed identity. Requires: Exchange Online. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Posts real-time Adaptive Cards to your SOC Teams channel with severity, affected users, exposure type, recommended actions, and direct incident links. Uses webhook (no Graph permissions needed). Post-deploy: add Teams webhook URL in Logic App parameters.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Auto-enriches every Sentinel incident with SpyCloud context: breach source, historical exposure count, password reuse analysis, malware family, and infected device info. Adds tags like 'malware-sourced', 'plaintext-password'. Requires: Sentinel Responder role. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Master orchestrator that chains ALL playbooks in 3 phases: Phase 1 (parallel): password reset + session revocation + device isolation → Phase 2 (parallel): MFA enforcement + CA group assignment → Phase 3: SOC notification. Best used as the single automation rule target for severity 25 (session cookie) incidents. Requires: all individual playbook licenses. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.
Enable ServiceNow integration for automatic ticket creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the serviceNow* parameters below.
ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.service-now.com). Required when enableServiceNow is TRUE.
Authentication method for the ServiceNow API connection. Basic = username/password, OAuth = OAuth 2.0 client credentials, ApiKey = ServiceNow API key header.
ServiceNow username for Basic authentication. Must have permissions to create records in the target table. Required when serviceNowAuthType is 'Basic'.
ServiceNow password for Basic authentication. Stored securely and never exposed in deployment outputs or logs. Required when serviceNowAuthType is 'Basic'.

ServiceNow table to create records in. 'incident' = standard IT incidents, 'sn_si_incident' = Security Incident Response (requires SIR plugin), 'change_request' = change records, 'problem' = problem records.
ServiceNow assignment group name for created tickets (e.g., 'SOC Team', 'Security Operations'). Leave blank to use the default assignment group for the target table.
ServiceNow ticket category for created records. 'Security' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure incidents.

Template for the ServiceNow short description field. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}, {severity_label}. Example: 'SpyCloud: {severity} exposure detected for {email}'.

JSON string of custom ServiceNow field mappings. Use to set additional fields not covered by the standard parameters. Example: {"u_risk_score": "{severity}", "u_source": "SpyCloud"}. Leave as '{}' if no custom fields are needed.
Enable Jira integration for automatic issue creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the jira* parameters below.
Jira instance URL (e.g., https://your-org.atlassian.net). Required when enableJira is TRUE.
Authentication method for the Jira API connection. ApiToken = email + API token (Jira Cloud), OAuth = OAuth 2.0, PAT = Personal Access Token (Jira Data Center/Server).
Jira account email for ApiToken authentication. Must have permissions to create issues in the target project. Required when jiraAuthType is 'ApiToken'.
Jira API token for authentication. Generate at: https://id.atlassian.net/manage-profile/security/api-tokens. Stored securely and never exposed in deployment outputs or logs.
Jira project key where issues will be created (e.g., 'SEC', 'SOC', 'SECOPS'). The project must already exist in your Jira instance.
Jira issue type for created issues. 'Task' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure response work items.
Comma-separated Jira labels applied to created issues. Used for filtering and reporting. Example: 'spycloud,credential-exposure,automated'.

Template for the Jira issue summary field. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud] {severity_label} credential exposure - {email}'.

JSON string of custom Jira field mappings. Use to set additional fields not covered by standard parameters. Example: {"customfield_10001": "{severity}", "customfield_10002": "SpyCloud"}. Leave as '{}' if no custom fields are needed.
Enable Azure DevOps integration for automatic work item creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the azureDevOps* parameters below.
Azure DevOps organization URL (e.g., https://dev.azure.com/your-org). Required when enableAzureDevOps is TRUE.
Azure DevOps project name where work items will be created. The project must already exist in your Azure DevOps organization. Required when enableAzureDevOps is TRUE.
Azure DevOps work item type for created items. 'Issue' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure tracking in Basic process; use 'Task' or 'Bug' for Agile/Scrum processes.
Azure DevOps area path for created work items (e.g., 'ProjectName\Security\Incidents'). Leave blank to use the project root area path.
Azure DevOps iteration path for created work items (e.g., 'ProjectName\Sprint 1'). Leave blank to use the project default iteration.
Azure DevOps user email or display name to assign created work items to. Leave blank to leave unassigned.
Semicolon-separated tags applied to created Azure DevOps work items. Used for filtering and reporting.
Template for the Azure DevOps work item title. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud] {severity_label} - {email}'.
Enable enhanced Slack notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted alert messages to a designated Slack channel via webhook. When enabled, configure the slack* parameters below.
Slack channel for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., '#soc-alerts'). The Slack app must be invited to this channel. Required when enableSlackNotifications is TRUE.
Display name for the Slack bot that posts SpyCloud alert notifications.
Slack message format. 'Rich' = Block Kit formatted messages with sections, fields, and action buttons. 'Simple' = plain text messages with minimal formatting.
Filter which incidents are sent to Slack by severity. 'All' = all severities, 'HighCritical' = High and Critical only, 'CriticalOnly' = Critical severity only. Use to reduce notification noise.
Include compromised user details (email, department, manager) in Slack notifications. Set to FALSE if your Slack channel has broad visibility and PII should be minimized.
Enable enhanced Microsoft Teams notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted cards to a designated Teams channel. When enabled, configure the teams* parameters below.
Microsoft Teams channel name for SpyCloud alert notifications. The Teams webhook must be configured for this channel. Required when enableTeamsNotifications is TRUE.
Teams card format. 'AdaptiveCard' = modern rich cards with actions and dynamic content (recommended). 'MessageCard' = legacy Office 365 connector card format for backward compatibility.
Filter which incidents are sent to Teams by severity. 'All' = all severities, 'HighCritical' = High and Critical only, 'CriticalOnly' = Critical severity only.
Include actionable remediation buttons in Teams Adaptive Cards (e.g., 'Reset Password', 'Revoke Sessions', 'View in Sentinel'). Only applicable when teamsCardFormat is 'AdaptiveCard'.

Enable enhanced email notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted HTML emails with breach details, affected user information, and recommended remediation actions. When enabled, configure the email* parameters below.

Sender email address for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., 'sentinel-alerts@company.com'). Must be a valid mailbox in your Office 365 or Exchange Online tenant. Required when enableEmailNotifications is TRUE.
Comma-separated list of recipient email addresses for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., 'soc@company.com,security-leads@company.com'). Required when enableEmailNotifications is TRUE.
Comma-separated list of CC email addresses for SpyCloud alert notifications. Leave blank if no CC recipients are needed.
Template for the email subject line. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud Alert] {severity_label} - {email}'.
Include detailed breach source information in email notifications (breach name, date, data types exposed). Provides SOC analysts with full context for triage.
Include recommended remediation actions in email notifications based on the exposure type and severity (e.g., password reset, session revocation, MFA enforcement, device scan).

REQUIRED — Your primary corporate domain to monitor across all SpyCloud APIs (e.g., 'example.com'). Used by Investigations, IDLINK, Data Partnership, Exposure, and CAP connectors to query domain-scoped data. Also used by SIP if sipCookieDomain is not set separately.

SpyCloud Enterprise/Compass API Key — used for Breach Watchlist, Breach Catalog, Identity Exposure, and Compass endpoints. Only required if you are deploying Enterprise/Compass data sources. Leave blank if you are only deploying standalone products (Investigations, IDLink, etc.) with their own dedicated keys. Enterprise and Compass share this single key. Obtain from portal.spycloud.com > Settings > API Keys.
OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Compass connectors (deep infostealer investigation data, device fingerprints, stolen applications). Requires Enterprise+ API subscription and the Enterprise/Compass API key.
DEPRECATED: Compass uses the same API key as Enterprise. Leave blank — the Enterprise/Compass apiKey parameter is automatically used for all Compass endpoints.

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Session Identity Protection (SIP) connector for stolen cookie and session token ingestion. Requires a SIP API subscription. WARNING: Enabling this with an API key that lacks SIP entitlements will cause 403 Forbidden errors.

API key for SpyCloud SIP (Session Identity Protection) endpoints. Required if deploying SIP as a standalone product. If you have Enterprise/Compass enabled and leave this blank, the Enterprise API key is used as fallback.
REQUIRED when enableSip is TRUE: The cookie domain to monitor for stolen session cookies (e.g., 'example.com'). SpyCloud will return all stolen cookies for this domain and its subdomains.

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Investigations connector for enriched email-based investigation data. Requires an Investigations API subscription. WARNING: Enabling this with an API key that lacks Investigations entitlements will cause 403 Forbidden errors.

API key for SpyCloud Investigations endpoints. Required if deploying Investigations as a standalone product. If you have Enterprise/Compass enabled and leave this blank, the Enterprise API key is used as fallback.

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud IDLINK connector for identity correlation and graph analysis. IDLINK correlates fragmented identity data across breaches to build unified identity graphs. Requires IDLINK API entitlement and a dedicated API key.
REQUIRED when enableIdLink is TRUE: Dedicated API key for the SpyCloud IDLINK service. This is a separate key from your Enterprise key.
OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Data Partnership connector for partner-contributed darknet intelligence. Requires Data Partnership API entitlement and a dedicated API key.
REQUIRED when enableDataPartnership is TRUE: Dedicated API key for the SpyCloud Data Partnership service.

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Exposure connector for aggregated exposure risk assessments with scoring, timeline analysis, and remediation recommendations. Requires Exposure API entitlement and a dedicated API key.
REQUIRED when enableExposure is TRUE: Dedicated API key for the SpyCloud Exposure service.

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud CAP (Credential Access Program) connector for automated credential access protection policies, automated password resets, and credential lifecycle management. Requires CAP API entitlement and a dedicated API key.
REQUIRED when enableCAP is TRUE: Dedicated API key for the SpyCloud CAP service.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploy SpyCloud enrichment playbooks that call the SpyCloud API at incident time to get fresh, entity-specific breach data. Enrichment playbooks add investigation context (breach source, severity, affected domains, device forensics) directly to incident comments. Requires the Enterprise API key provided above.

Deploy the Email Enrichment playbook. When a SpyCloud incident fires with an Account (email) entity, this playbook calls /breach/data/emails/{email} to get all breach records for that email. Results include: total exposures, severity distribution, plaintext passwords found, target domains affected, infection details. Written to incident comment + enrichment audit table.

Deploy the Domain Enrichment playbook. When a SpyCloud incident fires with a DNS domain entity, this playbook calls /breach/data/domains/{domain} to get all breach records for that domain. Results include: total exposed users, severity breakdown, top exposed accounts, breach sources.

Deploy the IP Enrichment playbook. When a SpyCloud incident fires with an IP address entity, this playbook calls /breach/data/ips/{ip} to get breach records associated with that IP. Results include: infected devices at this IP, affected users, malware families, infection dates.
Deploy the Username Enrichment playbook. Calls /breach/data/usernames/{username} for non-email identifiers. Useful for service account or non-email identity compromise.

Deploy the Catalog Enrichment playbook. Enriches incidents with breach source context by calling /breach/catalog/{source_id}. Returns: breach title, description, acquisition date, record count, confidence score, type (public/private).

Deploy Compass enrichment playbooks. Requires Compass API key. Calls /compass/data/emails/{email} and /compass/devices/{machine_id} for consumer exposure data, application-level stolen credentials, and device fingerprints.

Deploy the Stolen Cookies enrichment playbook. Requires SIP API key. Calls /sip/cookies/emails/{email} to find stolen session cookies for the user. Reports affected applications, cookie types, MFA bypass risk.

Deploy Investigation enrichment playbooks. Requires Investigations API key. Provides full-database deep investigation using /investigations-v2/records/. Returns all available data including historical exposures, linked identities, full PII profiles.

Deploy the Full Investigation orchestration playbook. Chains multiple enrichment steps: email lookup → catalog enrichment → Compass device (if available) → SIP cookie check (if available) → MDE correlation → Entra sign-in correlation. Each step is conditional — gracefully skips products without API keys. Rate limited: max 1/minute, 50/day.

Maximum number of enrichment API calls per day across all playbooks. Prevents exceeding SpyCloud API quotas. Default: 200 (handles ~50-100 incidents/day). Increase for high-volume environments after confirming with SpyCloud support.

connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/docs/images/spycloud-wordmark-200.png)

**Darknet Identity Breach Intelligence for Microsoft Sentinel**

SpyCloud recaptures stolen credentials, session cookies, and infected device data from the criminal underground — hours after exposure, not weeks after disclosure.

**This connector ingests identity threat intelligence across 15 custom tables:**

**Core (Enterprise API — always active):**
- **Breach Watchlist** (84 fields) — credential exposures with full PII and device forensics
- **Breach Catalog** (29 fields) — breach metadata, malware families, targeted industries

**Compass — Endpoint Threat Protection (requires Compass license):**
- **Compass Data** (32 fields) — consumer identity exposures
- **Compass Devices** (10 fields) — infected device inventory
- **Compass Applications** (9 fields) — compromised application statistics

**Advanced (requires separate licenses):**
- **SIP Cookies** (22 fields) — stolen session cookies for MFA bypass detection
- **Investigations** (84 fields) — full database access for threat hunting
- **IdLink** (15 fields) — identity correlation across personas
- **Exposure Stats** (12 fields) — domain exposure metrics and trends
- **CAP** (40 fields) — consumer compromised account data
- **Data Partnership** (50 fields) — partner intelligence feeds

**Also deployed:** Enrichment Audit, MDE Response Logs, Conditional Access Logs.

For more information, visit [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com) or contact [integrations@spycloud.com](mailto:integrations@spycloud.com).

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Exposure Detected
SpyCloud — Infostealer Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects when SpyCloud identifies credentials stolen by infostealer malware (severity 20+). These are active infections where malware on a device has exfiltrated passwords, cookies, and session tokens. Requires immediate device investigation and credential reset. Creates an incident with the exposed email as an account entity.
properties.query:
[concat('SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= ', string(parameters('spycloudSeverityThreshold')), '
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend
    HostCustomEntity = user_hostname,
    AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])')]

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no SpyCloud data has been received for more than 2 hours, indicating a potential connector failure or API key issue.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(LastRecord:datetime)[]) , (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated) | where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Credential Exposure Detected
SpyCloud — Infostealer Credential Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects new infostealer-sourced credential exposures (severity >= 20) from SpyCloud. These indicate a device was actively infected with malware that stole credentials, cookies, and session tokens. Severity 25 includes stolen application data enabling MFA bypass.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), MaxSeverity = max(severity) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Plaintext Password Exposure (Critical Risk)
SpyCloud — Plaintext Password Exposure (Critical Risk)
properties.description: Alerts when SpyCloud finds credentials with plaintext (unencrypted) passwords. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without any cracking. Highest priority for password reset. Check if the password is reused across other services.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — SSN / Financial / Health Data Exposure (Compliance Alert)
SpyCloud — SSN / Financial / Health Data Exposure (Compliance Alert)
properties.description: Detects exposure records containing Social Security numbers, bank account numbers, tax IDs, or health insurance IDs. May trigger breach notification obligations under HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, or state privacy laws.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(social_security_number) or isnotempty(ssn_last_four) or isnotempty(bank_number) or isnotempty(taxid) or isnotempty(health_insurance_id)
| summarize PIITypes = make_set(case(isnotempty(social_security_number), 'SSN-Full', isnotempty(ssn_last_four), 'SSN-Last4', isnotempty(bank_number), 'BankAccount', isnotempty(taxid), 'TaxID', isnotempty(health_insurance_id), 'HealthInsurance', 'Other'), 10), ExposureCount = count() by email, full_name
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Stolen Session Cookies / Tokens (MFA Bypass Risk)
SpyCloud — Stolen Session Cookies / Tokens (MFA Bypass Risk)
properties.description: Detects when stolen browser cookies or session tokens are found in SpyCloud data. Attackers can use these to bypass MFA entirely by replaying the authenticated session. Requires immediate session revocation and device investigation. This is the most dangerous exposure type.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity == 25
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 15), Devices = make_set(infected_machine_id, 5) by email, user_hostname
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Device Re-Infection Detected
SpyCloud — Device Re-Infection Detected
properties.description: Detects when the same infected_machine_id appears in new SpyCloud exposure records after a previous remediation was logged in the MDE table. Indicates the device was re-compromised after initial remediation, suggesting incomplete cleanup or persistent malware.
properties.query:
let remediated_devices = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | extend _IsolationStatus = ActionStatus | where _IsolationStatus == true or ActionStatus == "Succeeded" | distinct InfectedMachineId;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where infected_machine_id in (remediated_devices)
| summarize NewExposures = count(), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, email
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — User Credentials Exposed Across Multiple Domains
SpyCloud — User Credentials Exposed Across Multiple Domains
properties.description: Detects when a single user has credentials stolen for 5 or more different target domains, indicating widespread credential reuse or a heavily infected device. High risk of account takeover across multiple services.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize TargetDomainCount = dcount(target_domain), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), MaxSeverity = max(severity), HasPlaintext = countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)) by email
| where TargetDomainCount >= 5
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infection from Unusual Geographic Location
SpyCloud — Infection from Unusual Geographic Location
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections from country codes that are uncommon in your environment (fewer than 3 previous infections from that country). May indicate targeted attacks, compromised travel devices, or unauthorized access from unexpected locations.
properties.query:
let country_baseline = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | summarize DeviceCount = dcount(infected_machine_id) by country_code | where DeviceCount >= 3 | distinct country_code;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(country_code)
| where country_code !in (country_baseline)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by country_code, user_hostname, infected_machine_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Credential Observed in Multiple Breach Sources
SpyCloud — Credential Observed in Multiple Breach Sources
properties.description: Detects credentials with a sighting count of 3 or more, meaning the same email/password combination has been observed across multiple independent breach sources. This dramatically increases the probability of active credential stuffing attacks using this pair.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where sighting >= 3
| summarize MaxSighting = max(sighting), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), PasswordTypes = make_set(password_type, 5), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — High-Severity Exposure Without Automated Remediation
SpyCloud — High-Severity Exposure Without Automated Remediation
properties.description: Detects severity 20+ infostealer exposures that have been in the watchlist for over 2 hours but have NO corresponding entry in the Conditional Access remediation logs. This means the automated password reset playbook did not trigger, leaving the compromised credential active.
properties.query:
let remediated_users = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct Email;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(2h)
| where email !in (remediated_users)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), MaxSeverity = max(severity), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10) by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Antivirus Present But Failed to Prevent Infostealer
SpyCloud — Antivirus Present But Failed to Prevent Infostealer
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections on devices that had antivirus software installed, indicating the AV product failed to prevent the malware. Useful for evaluating endpoint protection effectiveness and identifying products that need updated signatures or configuration changes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(av_softwares)
| mv-expand av_softwares
| summarize InfectedDevices = dcount(infected_machine_id), AffectedUsers = dcount(email) by tostring(av_softwares)
| where InfectedDevices >= 2

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Malware Family Appeared in Breach Catalog
SpyCloud — New Malware Family Appeared in Breach Catalog
properties.description: Fires when a new breach catalog entry appears with a status of 'pending' (newly ingested). This means SpyCloud has identified a new breach or malware source that may contain your organization's data. Early awareness enables proactive response before full ingestion completes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where status == 'pending'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, source_id, breach_title, description, status

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no new SpyCloud watchlist data has been received for more than 3 hours, indicating a potential connector failure, API key expiration, or rate limiting issue. Check SentinelHealth table and the SpyCloud connector page for errors.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated)
| where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord)
| extend AlertMessage = strcat('Last SpyCloud data received: ', tostring(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Okta — Compromised Credential Used in Okta Sign-In
SpyCloud × Okta — Compromised Credential Used in Okta Sign-In
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed email addresses with Okta sign-in events in Sentinel. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials (from SpyCloud) successfully authenticates through Okta, indicating the stolen credentials may be actively exploited. Requires Okta logs ingested via the Okta SSO connector in Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Okta_CL
| where eventType_s == 'user.session.start' and outcome_result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where actor_alternateId_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = actor_alternateId_s, IPCustomEntity = client_ipAddress_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Duo — Compromised Credential in Duo Authentication
SpyCloud × Duo — Compromised Credential in Duo Authentication
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Cisco Duo authentication logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials authenticates through Duo. Requires Duo Admin API logs ingested into Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Duo_CL
| where result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where email_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email_s, IPCustomEntity = access_device_ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Ping Identity — Compromised Credential in PingOne Auth
SpyCloud × Ping Identity — Compromised Credential in PingOne Auth
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Ping Identity/PingOne authentication logs. Requires PingOne logs ingested into Sentinel via syslog or API connector.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
PingFederate_CL
| where status_s == 'success'
| where subject_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = subject_s, IPCustomEntity = ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Entra ID — Compromised Credential Sign-In Detected
SpyCloud × Entra ID — Compromised Credential Sign-In Detected
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials from SpyCloud successfully signs in, especially from unusual locations or with anomalous patterns. Requires Entra ID sign-in logs enabled (enableEntraSignInLogs=true).
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (compromised_users)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), Locations = make_set(Location, 5), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5) by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (exposed)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5), Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5) by UserPrincipalName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Exposures with Breach Source Context
SpyCloud — New Exposures with Breach Source Context
properties.description: Enriches new SpyCloud watchlist records with breach catalog details (breach title, description) by joining on source_id. Fires when new high-severity exposures arrive from known breach sources, providing SOC analysts with the full context of where the data came from.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where severity >= 20
| join kind=leftouter (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | project source_id, breach_title, description, status) on source_id
| where isnotempty(breach_title)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by uuid, breach_title, status
| extend AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Executive / VIP Account Exposure
SpyCloud — Executive / VIP Account Exposure
properties.description: Detects when VIP/executive accounts appear in SpyCloud data. Uses the SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist (editable in Sentinel → Watchlists) for dynamic matching. Falls back to common executive patterns (ceo, cfo, cto, ciso, admin, root) if watchlist is empty. Edit the watchlist to add your organization's specific executives.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Same Password Reused Across Multiple Target Domains
SpyCloud — Same Password Reused Across Multiple Target Domains
properties.description: Detects when the same password hash appears in exposures targeting 3 or more different domains for the same user. This indicates dangerous credential reuse — if one account is compromised, all accounts with the same password are at risk.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password) or isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| extend pw_hash = hash_sha256(coalesce(password_plaintext, password))
| summarize TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), DomainCount = dcount(target_domain) by email, pw_hash
| where DomainCount >= 3
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Exposure Older Than 7 Days Without Remediation
SpyCloud — Exposure Older Than 7 Days Without Remediation
properties.description: Detects high-severity (20+) exposures that have been in the watchlist for more than 7 days but have no corresponding entry in either the MDE or CA remediation logs. This is a compliance and SLA alert — these exposures should have been remediated within your organization's defined response window.
properties.query:
let mde_remediated = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | distinct InfectedMachineId;
let ca_remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct Email;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(7d)
| where infected_machine_id !in (mde_remediated)
| where email !in (ca_remediated)
| summarize DaysExposed = datetime_diff('day', now(), min(TimeGenerated)), ExposureCount = count() by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| distinct email, target_domain;
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where Name has_any (exposed | project target_domain)
| join kind=inner exposed on $left.Name == $right.target_domain
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, Name, email, target_domain, QueryType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| mv-expand ip = ip_addresses
| extend src_ip = tostring(ip)
| where isnotempty(src_ip)
| distinct src_ip, email, infected_machine_id;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where RemoteIP in~ (ips | project src_ip)
| join kind=inner ips on $left.RemoteIP == $right.src_ip
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, email, infected_machine_id

| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 and isnotempty(user_hostname)
| distinct user_hostname, email, severity, infected_machine_id;
DeviceInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| join kind=inner devices on $left.DeviceName == $right.user_hostname
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, DeviceId, OSPlatform, email, severity, infected_machine_id

| where upn in~ (exposed)
| project TimeGenerated, AlertId, Title, Severity, upn, Category

let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
EmailEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where RecipientEmailAddress in~ (exposed) and ThreatTypes has 'Phish' | project TimeGenerated, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, ThreatTypes

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Identity — Lateral Movement by Exposed User
SpyCloud × Identity — Lateral Movement by Exposed User
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Identity — Lateral Movement by Exposed User
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
IdentityLogonEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where AccountUpn in~ (exposed) and LogonType in ('Interactive','RemoteInteractive') | where DestinationDeviceName != DeviceName | project TimeGenerated, AccountUpn, DeviceName, DestinationDeviceName, LogonType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × TI — Infection IPs in Threat Intelligence
SpyCloud × TI — Infection IPs in Threat Intelligence
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × TI — Infection IPs in Threat Intelligence
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and isnotempty(NetworkIP) | where NetworkIP in (ips) | project TimeGenerated, NetworkIP, ThreatType, ConfidenceScore, Description

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × UEBA — Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
SpyCloud × UEBA — Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × UEBA — Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email;
BehaviorAnalytics | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed) | where ActivityInsights has 'True' or InvestigationPriority > 3 | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, ActivityType, InvestigationPriority

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Azure — Exposed User Modifying Cloud Resources
SpyCloud × Azure — Exposed User Modifying Cloud Resources
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Azure — Exposed User Modifying Cloud Resources
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
AzureActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where Caller in~ (exposed) | where OperationNameValue has 'write' or OperationNameValue has 'delete' | where ActivityStatusValue == 'Success' | project TimeGenerated, Caller, OperationNameValue, ResourceGroup

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Compass — Consumer + Corporate Cross-Reference
SpyCloud Compass — Consumer + Corporate Cross-Reference
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud Compass — Consumer + Corporate Cross-Reference
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassData_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | distinct email, severity) on email | project TimeGenerated, email, breach_title, target_domain, CompassSev=severity, CorporateSev=severity1

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Firewall — Infected IP in Security Logs
SpyCloud × Firewall — Infected IP in Security Logs
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Firewall — Infected IP in Security Logs
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
CommonSecurityLog | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d) | where SourceIP in (ips) or DestinationIP in (ips) | project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, DestinationIP, DeviceAction, DeviceVendor

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Impossible Travel — Sign-In from Unexpected Country
SpyCloud × Impossible Travel — Sign-In from Unexpected Country
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Impossible Travel — Sign-In from Unexpected Country
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email, country_code;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) and ResultType == 0 | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed | project email) | extend cc = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion) | join kind=inner (exposed | project email, sc_cc=country_code) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | where cc != sc_cc and isnotempty(cc) | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, cc, sc_cc, IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Compass — Device Reinfection Detected
SpyCloud Compass — Device Reinfection Detected
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud Compass — Device Reinfection Detected
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | join kind=inner (SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | where TimeGenerated between(ago(60d) .. ago(7d)) | distinct user_hostname, user_os) on user_hostname | project TimeGenerated, user_hostname, user_os, application_count, ip_addresses

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Entra ID Protection alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents when Entra ID Protection detects risky users or risky sign-ins. Correlate with SpyCloud data to determine if the risky user was flagged due to darknet credential exposure.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Defender for Cloud alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Cloud (Azure Defender) alerts covering VMs, SQL, Storage, Key Vault, App Service, Kubernetes, and more. Cross-correlate with SpyCloud to detect compromised credentials accessing cloud resources.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Microsoft Cloud App Security alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MCAS) alerts. Detects impossible travel, suspicious inbox rules, OAuth app abuse, and mass file downloads — correlate with SpyCloud to verify if compromised credentials were used.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Defender for Identity alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Identity alerts (on-premises Active Directory). Detects pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync, and reconnaissance activities — critical to correlate with SpyCloud credential exposures that may enable these attacks.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Enrich and Tag Credential Exposure Incidents
properties.description: Automatically sets severity and adds investigation tasks for SpyCloud credential exposure incidents. To auto-trigger playbooks, configure the RunPlaybook action manually after granting Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor role to the playbook's managed identity.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud VIP/Executive Watchlist
properties.description: Editable list of VIP and executive email addresses for elevated SpyCloud alerting. Add emails to this watchlist and Rule #20 (Executive/VIP Exposure) will dynamically match against it. Edit in Sentinel → Watchlists → SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist.
properties.rawContent:
Email,Name,Title,Priority
ceo@company.com,CEO,Chief Executive Officer,Critical
cfo@company.com,CFO,Chief Financial Officer,Critical
cto@company.com,CTO,Chief Technology Officer,Critical
ciso@company.com,CISO,Chief Information Security Officer,Critical
admin@company.com,IT Admin,Domain Administrator,High

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Exposed Users with Active Sign-Ins
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email, severity, breach_title;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) and ResultType == 0 | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed | project email) | join kind=inner exposed on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | summarize SignIns=count(), Apps=dcount(AppDisplayName), IPs=dcount(IPAddress) by UserPrincipalName, severity, breach_title | order by severity desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Finds users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud AND who have successful Entra ID sign-ins in the last 48 hours. These are the highest-priority accounts — an attacker may already be using the stolen credentials.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Infected IPs Across All Network Logs
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
union isfuzzy=true
(DeviceNetworkEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where RemoteIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by RemoteIP, DeviceName | extend Source="MDE"),
(DnsEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ClientIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by ClientIP | extend Source="DNS"),
(CommonSecurityLog | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where SourceIP in (ips) or DestinationIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by SourceIP, DestinationIP | extend Source="Firewall"),
(ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | where NetworkIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by NetworkIP, ThreatType | extend Source="TI")
| order by Hits desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Sweeps MDE network events, DNS, firewall (CEF), and Threat Intelligence feeds for any IP addresses associated with SpyCloud infostealer infections. Comprehensive network-level threat hunt.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Password Reuse Across Domains
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and isnotempty(password) | summarize Domains=dcount(target_domain), DomainList=make_set(target_domain, 10), Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity) by email, password | where Domains >= 3 | project email, Domains, DomainList, Exposures, MaxSev | order by Domains desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies users whose same password hash appears across 3+ different target domains — indicating dangerous password reuse. These users are vulnerable to credential stuffing attacks across all their accounts.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Devices Infected Multiple Times
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) and isnotempty(infected_machine_id) | summarize Infections=count(), Sources=dcount(source_id), Users=dcount(email), FirstSeen=min(spycloud_publish_date), LastSeen=max(spycloud_publish_date) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, user_os | where Infections > 1 | order by Infections desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Finds devices that appear in SpyCloud data across multiple breach sources or time periods — indicating the device was not properly remediated after the first infection, or the user continues risky behavior.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Plaintext Passwords by Domain
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and isnotempty(password_plaintext) | summarize PlaintextCount=count(), Users=dcount(email), Domains=dcount(target_domain), MaxSev=max(severity) by email_domain | order by PlaintextCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Aggregates all plaintext (cleartext) password exposures by email domain. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without any cracking. Priority for password reset and user notification.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Compass Consumer + Corporate Overlap
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassData_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and severity >= 20 | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | distinct email, severity) on email | summarize CompassHits=count(), CorporateMaxSev=max(severity1), CompassMaxSev=max(severity), Domains=dcount(target_domain) by email | order by CorporateMaxSev desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Cross-references SpyCloud Compass (consumer identity) data with corporate watchlist data. Users appearing in both datasets are reusing personal credentials for corporate accounts — a major risk vector.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Unremediated High-Severity Exposures
properties.query:
let remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | distinct tolower(Email);
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | where tolower(email) !in (remediated) | summarize Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity), Domains=dcount(target_domain), LatestExposure=max(TimeGenerated) by email | order by MaxSev desc, Exposures desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies high-severity (20+) exposures that have NOT been remediated by the CA playbook. These are the most urgent action items — exposed credentials without a password reset.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Risk Score Dashboard
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | summarize Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity), PlaintextPWs=countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)), Devices=dcountif(infected_machine_id, isnotempty(infected_machine_id)), Domains=dcount(target_domain), DaysSince=datetime_diff('day',now(),max(TimeGenerated)) by email | extend Score = Exposures*2 + MaxSev*3 + PlaintextPWs*10 + Devices*15 + iff(DaysSince<7,20,iff(DaysSince<30,10,0)) | extend Risk=case(Score>=100,"CRITICAL",Score>=50,"HIGH",Score>=20,"MEDIUM","LOW") | order by Score desc | take 100

tags.name: description
tags.value: Calculates a composite risk score for every exposed user based on severity, plaintext passwords, device infections, target domain count, and recency. Use to prioritize remediation efforts.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Escalate Critical Severity Incidents
properties.description: Automatically sets severity to High and assigns owner tag for SpyCloud incidents involving severity 25 (critical infostealer) or plaintext password exposure. Ensures immediate SOC attention.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Task Device Infection Incidents
properties.description: Automatically adds investigation tasks for incidents involving infected devices or device reinfection patterns detected by SpyCloud Compass.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Close Low-Risk Informational Alerts
properties.description: Automatically closes informational-severity incidents (e.g., new malware family in catalog, AV-present-but-failed) after tagging them for tracking. Reduces SOC noise while preserving audit trail.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud IOC Blocklist
properties.description: Indicators of Compromise collected from SpyCloud infostealer data — malicious IPs, domains, and URLs associated with credential theft infrastructure. Used by hunting queries and analytics rules for automated blocking. Add entries manually or via the MDE Blocklist playbook.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Approved/Corporate Domains
properties.description: List of approved corporate email domains for SpyCloud monitoring scope. Analytics rules reference this watchlist to focus alerting on corporate accounts vs. personal accounts. Add all your organization's email domains here.
properties.rawContent:
Domain,Type,Priority,Notes
company.com,Primary,Critical,Main corporate domain
company.io,Secondary,High,Engineering domain

properties.displayName: SpyCloud High-Value Assets
properties.description: Critical servers, databases, and applications that require elevated monitoring when accessed by users with SpyCloud credential exposures. Used by hunting queries to prioritize investigation of compromised users accessing sensitive systems.
properties.rawContent:
AssetName,AssetType,Criticality,Owner,Notes
DC01,DomainController,Critical,IT-Infrastructure,Primary domain controller
SQL-PROD,Database,Critical,DBA-Team,Production SQL server
VPN-Gateway,Network,High,Network-Team,Corporate VPN concentrator
AzureAD-Connect,Identity,Critical,Identity-Team,Entra ID Connect sync server

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Stolen Session Cookies Used for MFA Bypass
properties.query:
let stolen_sessions = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | where isnotempty(infected_machine_id) and (isnotempty(cookie_domain) or log_id contains "cookie" or log_id contains "session") | distinct email, infected_machine_id, target_domain, severity;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and ResultType == 0 | where AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication" or TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD" | where UserPrincipalName in~ (stolen_sessions | project email) | join kind=inner stolen_sessions on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | summarize SessionReuses=count(), UniqueIPs=dcount(IPAddress), Countries=dcount(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion) by UserPrincipalName, severity, target_domain | where UniqueIPs > 1 | order by SessionReuses desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies users with stolen session cookies (from SpyCloud infostealer data) who have recent sign-ins bypassing MFA. Multiple unique IPs suggest token replay attacks.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Lateral Movement from Compromised Accounts
properties.query:
let compromised = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
IdentityLogonEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where AccountUpn in~ (compromised) | where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive", "Network", "CachedRemoteInteractive") | summarize UniqueTargets=dcount(DestinationDeviceName), Logons=count(), LogonTypes=make_set(LogonType) by AccountUpn, SourceDeviceName = DeviceName | where UniqueTargets > 2 | order by UniqueTargets desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Hunts for lateral movement by compromised users — accounts with SpyCloud exposure that authenticated to 3+ different machines via RDP, SMB, or remote logons. Indicates active use of stolen credentials.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Data Exfiltration After Credential Exposure
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email, spycloud_publish_date;
CloudAppEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ActionType in ("FileDownloaded", "FileSyncDownloadedFull", "FileModifiedExtended", "FileCopied") | where AccountObjectId != "" | join kind=inner (IdentityInfo | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | distinct AccountUPN, AccountObjectId) on AccountObjectId | where AccountUPN in~ (exposed | project email) | summarize Downloads=count(), UniqueFiles=dcount(ObjectName), DataVolumeMB=sum(toint(RawEventData.FileSizeBytes))/1048576 by AccountUPN, ActionType | where Downloads > 50 | order by Downloads desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Detects mass file downloads or data exfiltration from cloud apps by users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud. Tracks download volume and unique files to identify data theft post-compromise.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Mailbox Compromise and Forwarding Rules
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email;
OfficeActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where Operation in ("New-InboxRule", "Set-InboxRule", "Set-Mailbox", "Set-TransportRule") | where UserId in~ (exposed) | extend RuleDetails = tostring(Parameters) | where RuleDetails contains "Forward" or RuleDetails contains "Redirect" or RuleDetails contains "Delete" | project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, RuleDetails, ClientIP | order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Searches for mailbox forwarding rules, redirect rules, or deletion rules created by users with SpyCloud credential exposure. Common BEC and account takeover technique — attacker sets auto-forward to external mailbox.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Privilege Escalation by Compromised Users
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
AuditLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where OperationName in ("Add member to role", "Add eligible member to role", "Add owner to group", "Add member to group", "Consent to application", "Add app role assignment to service principal") | where InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName in~ (exposed) | project TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy=tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), Operation=OperationName, Target=tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName), Role=tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties[0].newValue) | order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Detects privilege escalation activities by users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud — role assignments, group additions, app consents, and service principal modifications. May indicate an attacker using stolen credentials to elevate access.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Malware Family Trends and Campaign Tracking
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) and severity >= 20 | where isnotempty(infected_path) or isnotempty(infected_machine_id) | extend malware_family = case(infected_path contains "redline", "RedLine", infected_path contains "raccoon", "Raccoon", infected_path contains "vidar", "Vidar", infected_path contains "lumma", "LummaC2", infected_path contains "stealc", "StealC", infected_path contains "aurora", "Aurora", infected_path contains "meta", "MetaStealer", infected_path contains "titan", "TitanStealer", "Unknown") | summarize Victims=dcount(email), Devices=dcount(infected_machine_id), FirstSeen=min(spycloud_publish_date), LastSeen=max(spycloud_publish_date), Domains=dcount(target_domain) by malware_family | order by Victims desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Tracks infostealer malware family trends from SpyCloud data — which malware families are targeting your organization, victim counts, device infections, and campaign timelines. Essential for threat intelligence reporting.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Third-Party Breach Impact Assessment
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) | summarize AffectedUsers=dcount(email), MaxSev=max(severity), PlaintextPWs=countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)), HasCookies=countif(isnotempty(infected_machine_id)) by source_id) on source_id | project BreachTitle=title, BreachDate=breach_date, AffectedUsers, MaxSev, PlaintextPWs, HasCookies, Description=description | order by AffectedUsers desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Assesses the organizational impact of each third-party breach or infostealer campaign in the SpyCloud catalog. Shows which breaches affect the most users, have plaintext passwords, or involve device infections.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Enriched Data Parser
properties.query:
let _lookback = coalesce(lookback, 1d);
let LatestBreachCatalog =
    SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
    | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, short_title, site_description, breach_main_category) by uuid
    | project
        uuid,
        catalog_title = short_title,
        catalog_description = site_description,
        catalog_category = breach_main_category;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(_lookback)
| extend source_uuid = tostring(source_id)
| join kind=leftouter LatestBreachCatalog on $left.source_uuid == $right.uuid
| project-away source_uuid, uuid

tags.name: description
tags.value: Joins SpyCloud Watchlist exposures with Breach Catalog metadata. Use: get_Spycloud_enriched_data(7d)

value.step1_mde_permissions: REQUIRED (if MDE playbook enabled): Assign Machine.Isolate + Machine.ReadWrite.All to the MDE Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step2_graph_permissions: REQUIRED (if CA playbook enabled): Assign User.ReadWrite.All + Directory.ReadWrite.All to the CA Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step3_sentinel_automation: REQUIRED (if automation rule enabled): Assign 'Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor' to the Sentinel service principal
value.step4_monitoring_publisher: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Assign 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' on the DCR to both Logic App identities
value.step5_defender_xdr: OPTIONAL: Enable Microsoft Defender XDR connector via Sentinel → Content Hub
value.step6_copilot_plugin: OPTIONAL: Upload copilot/SpyCloud_Plugin.yaml to Security Copilot → Sources → Custom (138 KQL skills)
value.step7_copilot_agent: OPTIONAL: Upload copilot/SpyCloud_Agent.yaml to Security Copilot → Build → Upload YAML (129 skills + GPT-4.1 analysis)
value.step0_dcr_dce: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Get the DCE Logs Ingestion URI and both DCR Immutable IDs (primary dcr-ccf-* and extended dcr-ccf-ext-*). Go to Azure Portal > Monitor > Data Collection Endpoints > copy Logs Ingestion URI. Then Monitor > Data Collection Rules > JSON View > copy immutableId for both DCRs. Update the Logic App parameters with these values, or redeploy the template with them filled in.

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh to auto-resolve DCE URI and DCR Immutable IDs (primary + extended)
ALL 38 rules are deployed DISABLED. Review each rule's KQL logic, then enable individually in Sentinel → Analytics. Additional 49 rules available in analytics-rules.json for manual import.
value.idpNote: Identity Provider correlation rules (Okta, Duo, Ping, Entra) require those IdP logs to be ingested into Sentinel first via their respective Content Hub connectors.

value.installInstructions: Upload each YAML file separately in Security Copilot > Sources > Manage Plugins > Custom > Add Plugin. Do NOT upload manifest.json.

value.okta: To ingest Okta logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Okta' → Install → Configure with Okta API token. Table: Okta_CL
value.duo: To ingest Duo logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Cisco Duo' → Install → Configure with Duo Admin API credentials. Table: Duo_CL
value.ping: To ingest Ping Identity logs: Configure syslog forwarding from PingFederate to Sentinel via AMA agent, or use the PingOne API connector. Table: PingFederate_CL
value.cyberark: To ingest CyberArk logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'CyberArk' → Install → Configure CEF/syslog forwarding. Table: CyberArk_CL
value.entraId: Entra ID sign-in/audit logs: Enable via this template's enableEntraSignInLogs and enableEntraAuditLogs parameters, or manually via Entra ID → Diagnostic Settings.
value.defenderXdr: Defender XDR: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft Defender XDR → Install & Connect. Provides AlertInfo, DeviceEvents, IdentityLogonEvents tables.
value.exchangeOnline: Exchange/O365: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft 365 → Install. Provides OfficeActivity, EmailEvents tables for phishing correlation.

value.note: Entra ID diagnostic settings CANNOT be deployed via ARM templates. Configure manually:
value.signInLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check SignInLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.auditLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check AuditLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.riskySignIns: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check RiskyUsers, UserRiskEvents → Send to Log Analytics workspace

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh after deployment to resolve DCE/DCR and assign RBAC + API permissions.
